context: Evaluates the implementation of the `derive_key` function that uses Argon2id to derive a 256-bit master key from a password and salt. The function must produce deterministic output for given inputs, use the Argon2id variant, and return the key in a `Zeroizing` wrapper that clears memory on drop. This function is the cryptographic foundation of the vsyncnotes vault.

checklist.name: Argon2id variant
checklist.description: Uses the Argon2id algorithm variant (not Argon2i or Argon2d) via `argon2::Argon2::default()` or explicit `Algorithm::Argon2id` configuration.

checklist.name: 32-byte output
checklist.description: Produces exactly 32 bytes of output key material (256 bits), correctly sized for use as an AES-256 key.

checklist.name: Deterministic derivation
checklist.description: Returns the same 32-byte key for identical password and salt inputs, and different keys when either input differs.

checklist.name: Zeroizing wrapper
checklist.description: Returns the derived key wrapped in `Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>` from the `zeroize` crate so that the key bytes are automatically set to zero when the value is dropped.

checklist.name: Error handling
checklist.description: Returns `Result<Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>, anyhow::Error>` and propagates Argon2 errors rather than panicking.
